content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Tom was very tired, ___ he went to bed early last night.
distractor_rationale: because introduces a cause clause, but here the tired feeling is the cause and going to bed is the result — the connector must introduce the result
due to must be followed by a noun phrase, not a full clause
since here would introduce a cause, not a result, reversing the logical direction of the sentence

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Maria missed the bus ___ she woke up late this morning.
distractor_rationale: so introduces a result clause, but waking up late is the cause of missing the bus, not the result
therefore introduces a result and cannot be placed mid-sentence between two clauses in this way
as a result introduces a result and requires a new sentence or semicolon boundary, not a mid-sentence connector

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The match was cancelled ___ the heavy snow on the field.
distractor_rationale: because must be followed by a full subject-verb clause, not a noun phrase like 'the heavy snow on the field'
so introduces a result clause and cannot be followed by a noun phrase explaining a cause
therefore introduces a result in a new clause and cannot be followed by a noun phrase explaining a cause

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The new road is very busy; ___, the local council has decided to build a second bridge nearby.
distractor_rationale: because introduces a cause clause and cannot follow a semicolon to introduce a result in this discourse structure
owing to must be followed by a noun phrase, not a full clause, and signals cause rather than result
since here would introduce a cause, not a result, and cannot follow a semicolon to present a logical consequence

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: ___ the café was fully booked, Anna and her friend decided to eat at the restaurant across the street instead.
distractor_rationale: therefore introduces a result and cannot open a sentence to explain why the following action was taken
as a result introduces a result clause and cannot begin a sentence that explains the reason for the action in the main clause
due to must be followed by a noun phrase, not a full subject-verb clause like 'the café was fully booked'

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The factory reduced its energy use by thirty per cent last year. ___, its electricity bills fell significantly.
distractor_rationale: because introduces a cause clause and cannot begin a sentence that presents the logical consequence of the previous sentence
owing to must be followed by a noun phrase and signals cause, not result, making it logically backwards here
as used as a cause connector must introduce a clause giving the reason for something, not the consequence of a preceding statement

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: James could not attend the meeting ___ a sudden problem with his flight connection.
distractor_rationale: because must be followed by a full subject-verb clause, not a noun phrase like 'a sudden problem with his flight connection'
so introduces a result clause and cannot be followed by a noun phrase that explains a cause
consequently introduces a result and cannot be followed by a noun phrase explaining why something happened

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The building project was delayed by several months ___ unexpected problems with the underground water pipes were discovered during construction.
distractor_rationale: due to must be followed by a noun phrase, not a full subject-verb clause beginning with 'unexpected problems … were discovered'
owing to must be followed by a noun phrase, not a full subject-verb clause, and so cannot precede this complete clause
therefore introduces a result and cannot introduce the reason why the building project was delayed

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Several key members of staff resigned last month; ___, the department head had to restructure the entire team before the project deadline.
distractor_rationale: because introduces a cause clause and cannot follow a semicolon to present the logical consequence of the preceding statement
as used as a cause connector introduces the reason for something and cannot follow a semicolon to present a result
because of must be followed by a noun phrase, not a full subject-verb clause, and signals cause rather than result

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: ___ the sudden rise in the cost of raw materials, the company was forced to increase its prices for the first time in five years.
distractor_rationale: because must be followed by a full subject-verb clause, not a noun phrase like 'the sudden rise in the cost of raw materials'
consequently introduces a result and cannot begin a sentence to explain the reason why the company increased its prices
so introduces a result clause in a compound sentence and cannot open a sentence followed by a noun phrase that gives a cause

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: Tom missed the bus, ___ he was late for work.
distractor_rationale: because introduces a cause clause, but the second clause here states the result of missing the bus, not the reason for it
due to must be followed by a noun phrase, not a full clause with subject and verb
since here would introduce the cause, but the second clause is the result, not the reason why he missed the bus

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The match was cancelled ___ the storm that hit the town overnight.
distractor_rationale: because must be followed by a full clause with a subject and verb, not a noun phrase like 'the storm that hit the town overnight'
so introduces a result clause and must be followed by a subject and verb, not a noun phrase
therefore introduces a result clause at the start of a new sentence or after a semicolon, and cannot be followed by a noun phrase expressing cause

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The hotel was fully booked for the weekend; ___, we had to find another place to stay.
distractor_rationale: because introduces a cause and must connect to a following clause explaining a reason, but the second clause here is the result, not the reason for the hotel being full
owing to must be followed by a noun phrase, not a full clause, and it introduces cause rather than result
although introduces a contrast or concession, which contradicts the logical result relationship between the two clauses here

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: The road was closed for three days ___ the flooding had damaged several sections of the surface.
distractor_rationale: due to must be followed by a noun phrase, not a full clause with a subject and verb such as 'the flooding had damaged several sections'
as a result introduces a result clause, but the second clause here explains the reason for the road closure, making it a cause, not a result
therefore introduces a result at the start of a new independent clause after a semicolon or full stop, and cannot be placed mid-sentence before a cause clause

content.instruction: Complete the sentence with the correct form.
content.prompt: ___ several members of the team were absent with illness, the project deadline was extended by one week.
distractor_rationale: due to must be followed by a noun phrase, not a full clause with a subject and verb such as 'several members of the team were absent with illness'
as a result introduces a consequence and must follow the cause that has already been stated; it cannot open a sentence that presents the cause first
so connects two clauses within a single sentence as a coordinating conjunction and cannot open a sentence or introduce a subordinate cause clause before the main clause

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The shop was closed, ___ we had to go home without the groceries.
distractor_rationale: 'due to' must be followed by a noun phrase, not a clause, so it cannot connect two full clauses here
'because' introduces a cause clause, but the second clause here is the result, not the cause
'since' here would make the second clause a cause, but 'we had to go home without the groceries' is the result, not a reason

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The flight was delayed ___ the bad weather conditions at the airport.
distractor_rationale: 'because' must be followed by a full clause with a subject and verb, not a noun phrase like 'the bad weather conditions'
'so' introduces a result clause and cannot precede a noun phrase explaining a cause
'therefore' introduces a result and cannot introduce a noun phrase giving a reason

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom had studied very hard for the exam; ___, he felt confident when he walked into the hall.
distractor_rationale: 'because' introduces a subordinate cause clause and cannot follow a semicolon to begin an independent result clause
'owing to' must be followed by a noun phrase, not a clause, and signals cause rather than result
'so' is a coordinating conjunction used between two clauses with a comma, not after a semicolon as a discourse connector

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The road was blocked ___ a serious accident, so all drivers had to take a different route.
distractor_rationale: 'because' requires a full clause with a subject and verb, but 'a serious accident' is only a noun phrase
'consequently' introduces a result, not a cause, and cannot precede a noun phrase
'since' requires a full clause with a subject and verb, not a bare noun phrase like 'a serious accident'

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The heating in the office broke down last night. ___, the manager told everyone to work from home today.
distractor_rationale: 'Because' at the start of a new sentence introduces a subordinate cause clause, not a result, and would make the sentence a fragment without a main clause
'Due to' must be followed by a noun phrase and signals cause, not result
'Since' introduces a cause clause and cannot begin an independent sentence that expresses a result

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: ___ the team had not practised enough, they made several mistakes during the final match.
distractor_rationale: 'Therefore' introduces a result clause and cannot begin a subordinate clause explaining why mistakes were made
'Due to' must be followed by a noun phrase, but 'the team had not practised enough' is a full clause
'As a result' introduces a result statement and cannot introduce the cause of the mistakes described in the main clause

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The project was completed two weeks ahead of schedule; ___, the client agreed to give the team a bonus.
distractor_rationale: 'because of' must be followed by a noun phrase and introduces a cause, not a result following a semicolon
'as' introduces a subordinate cause clause and cannot follow a semicolon to begin an independent result statement
'owing to' must be followed by a noun phrase and introduces a cause, not a result

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: The outdoor event was cancelled ___ the organisers had not arranged any shelter for the guests.
distractor_rationale: 'due to' must be followed by a noun phrase, but 'the organisers had not arranged any shelter' is a full clause with a subject and verb
'consequently' introduces a result and cannot introduce the cause of the cancellation
'therefore' introduces a result and cannot introduce a subordinate clause explaining why the event was cancelled

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Several key members of staff resigned last month. ___, the company has decided to pause its expansion plans until new staff are hired.
distractor_rationale: 'Because of' must be followed by a noun phrase and introduces a cause, not a result in a new sentence
'Since' introduces a subordinate cause clause and cannot begin an independent sentence expressing a consequence
'Owing to' must be followed by a noun phrase and introduces a cause, not a result expressed in a new independent sentence

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The bus was late, therefore we missed the first part of the film.
content.error_sentence: The bus was late, therefore we missed the first part of the film.
content.corrected_sentence: The bus was late, so we missed the first part of the film.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: She stayed home due to she had a bad cold and did not want to infect her colleagues.
content.error_sentence: She stayed home due to she had a bad cold and did not want to infect her colleagues.
content.corrected_sentence: She stayed home because she had a bad cold and did not want to infect her colleagues.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The match was cancelled because of the weather conditions were too dangerous for the players.
content.error_sentence: The match was cancelled because of the weather conditions were too dangerous for the players.
content.corrected_sentence: The match was cancelled because the weather conditions were too dangerous for the players.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: Tom had not slept well the night before, as a result he found it very hard to concentrate during the meeting.
content.error_sentence: Tom had not slept well the night before, as a result he found it very hard to concentrate during the meeting.
content.corrected_sentence: Tom had not slept well the night before, as a result, he found it very hard to concentrate during the meeting.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The road was closed because due to a large fallen tree, and drivers had to take a longer route.
content.correct_answer: because of a large fallen tree
content.error_sentence: The road was closed because due to a large fallen tree, and drivers had to take a longer route.
content.corrected_sentence: The road was closed because of a large fallen tree, and drivers had to take a longer route.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: Owing to the manager was away on a business trip, the team had to make all the decisions themselves.
content.error_sentence: Owing to the manager was away on a business trip, the team had to make all the decisions themselves.
content.corrected_sentence: Since the manager was away on a business trip, the team had to make all the decisions themselves.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: The project was delivered on time; consequently, the client was satisfied with the outcome. Therefore the team received a bonus.
content.correct_answer: As a result, the team received
content.error_sentence: The project was delivered on time; consequently, the client was satisfied with the outcome. Therefore the team received a bonus.
content.corrected_sentence: The project was delivered on time; consequently, the client was satisfied with the outcome. Therefore, the team received a bonus.

content.instruction: Find and correct the grammar mistake in the sentence.
content.prompt: Due to the heavy snowfall overnight, many schools were closed and, consequently, parents had to arrange childcare because of they could not go to work.
content.error_sentence: Due to the heavy snowfall overnight, many schools were closed and, consequently, parents had to arrange childcare because of they could not go to work.
content.corrected_sentence: Due to the heavy snowfall overnight, many schools were closed and, consequently, parents had to arrange childcare because they could not go to work.

content.instruction: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence.
content.prompt: Maria missed the bus ___ she woke up late this morning.
distractor_rationale: 'so' introduces a result clause, but 'she woke up late' is the cause, not the result
'therefore' introduces a result and cannot begin a subordinate cause clause
'due to' must be followed by a noun phrase, not a full clause like 'she woke up late'
